tasks.description: Perspective comparison view — new page at /w/[workspaceId]/perspectives/compare with two perspective selectors, comparison table showing annotations side-by-side, divergence highlighting (2+ point rating gap), summary stats, element navigation links, and sidebar nav entry
acceptance_criteria: New page at /w/[workspaceId]/perspectives/compare — only visible when 2+ perspectives exist with annotations
Two dropdown selectors at top to pick which perspectives to compare
Table layout: rows = annotated elements (steps/sections/touchpoints/stages), columns = each perspective's annotation text + rating
Divergence highlighting: rows where ratings differ by 2+ points get amber background
Summary stats at top: annotation count per perspective, average rating, divergence count, top 3 most divergent elements
Clicking element name navigates to that element on the correct canvas
Sidebar nav entry for Perspectives Compare (only shown when 2+ perspectives exist)

similar_files: src/app/(app)/w/[workspaceId]/compare/page.tsx — server-side data fetching pattern with Supabase
src/app/(app)/w/[workspaceId]/compare/compare-view.tsx — client comparison component pattern (stats overlays, name matching, export button)
patterns_to_follow: Server component page.tsx fetches data via createClient() + supabase queries, passes to client view component
Client component uses useState for selection, useMemo for computed data
fetchAnnotations(perspectiveId) from client.ts returns PerspectiveAnnotation[] — use for client-side cross-referencing
Sidebar NAV_ITEMS array with label, icon, href pattern — add entry between Compare and Teams
Use useWorkspace() for perspectives array (already in context)
Dark theme: bg-surface-primary, bg-surface-secondary, text-white/90, text-white/55, accent-blue for interactive
Divergence threshold should be a named constant: DIVERGENCE_THRESHOLD = 2
learnings_relevant: apiFetch unwraps envelope — result IS the data, not result.data

tasks.special_instructions: HUMAN-OVERRIDE: This is the first task of Phase 3a (Analysis Intelligence). Process FEEDBACK.md override: verify FEAT-031/032 deferred, verify STATUS.md updated, then build. Uses existing perspectives + perspective_annotations tables — NO migration needed. All entity names (steps, sections, stages, touchpoints) must be fetched server-side to populate the comparison table rows. For PDF export: add a simple button that calls a new comparison-perspective-pdf export function (similar pattern to comparison-pdf.ts). Consider decomposing into sub-tasks [1/2] core page + table + stats, [2/2] PDF export — but builder may do it in one pass if manageable.

tasks.description: Add hex color format validation to perspective API routes (POST and PATCH)
acceptance_criteria: PATCH /api/v1/perspectives/[id] validates color field if provided
Invalid color returns 400 with descriptive error message

similar_files: src/app/api/v1/annotations/route.ts — annotatable_type enum validation pattern (BUG-014 fix)
src/app/api/v1/annotations/[id]/route.ts — rating range validation pattern (BUG-015 fix)
patterns_to_follow: Guard clause before DB operation: if (color && !/^#[0-9A-Fa-f]{6}$/i.test(color)) return errorResponse('validation', 'Invalid color format. Expected hex color like #FF5733', 400)

tasks.special_instructions: Simple guard clause addition. Match the validation pattern from BUG-014 (annotatable_type validation) and BUG-015 (rating range validation).

shared_files.note: Task 1 (FEAT-033) owns sidebar.tsx. Task 2 (IMP-001) owns perspective API routes. No overlap.
